lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Good day friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I am Trobin Arceuus, lord of Arceuus. It's a position I've held for a thousand years, ever since the Ascent of Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a long time. I guess you've seen quite a bit.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Indeed. I've been present at some of the most notable moments in Kourend's history.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I was there when King Agata IV was deposed, I was there when Kharedst the Builder opened Kourend Castle and I was there when Emperor Imafore was executed.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I tend to keep out of public affairs these days though. My presence is often more of a distraction than a benefit. It also means I can focus on my work in peace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your work?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: The freeing of souls. There are many souls that find themselves trapped here after death. My people and I do our best to release them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: If you find something magical in Kourend, chances are it came from Arceuus. Our people have lived for a thousand years. The knowledge we've gained in that time has resulted in many a magical discovery.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Most of our discoveries start with the Dark Altar. It is the source of our immortality and our people dedicate their lives to studying it.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Our people also serves an important role in the wider world. We work to free trapped souls so that they may pass on to the next world. It is a crucial task that the Dark Altar helps us to perform.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was the Dark Altar always here?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Impossible to say. The Dark Altar has been mentioned in records dating back to the earliest days of Kourend. Before then, who knows.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You weren't around back then?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: No. The Ascent of Arceuus didn't occur until after Xeric's Age of Strife, hundreds of years after Kourend was formed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Ascent of Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: It was a thousand years ago, during the time of King Byrne II. I went to my people and shared the secrets of immortality with them.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Not all of them accepted this gift of course, as was their right. Those that did, were granted new bodies to house their souls. These bodies are sustained by the Dark Altar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wasn't you that discovered this secret though was it?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: No. It was granted to us by the Tasakaal. They are ancient beings, ones that make us seem like mere children.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Their age and experience have granted them knowledge over life and death that we can only dream of.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Tasakaal don't like you very much. They called you a liar and claimed you misused the knowledge.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: The events that led to the Ascent of Arceuus are more complicated than even the Tasakaal know. But that is a story for another day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about your plans.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I had one of my people recover the remains of that device. The one that was absorbing the Dark Altars power.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: We're performing tests to see if we can learn more about it. We haven't made any meaningful progress yet though.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Once we've learnt more about it, we'll be in a better position to determine our next move.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I admit, the idea of someone sabotaging the Altar is more than a bit concerning. I hope we can make some progress soon.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Now that we know Xamphur was responsible for the device that was absorbing power from the Dark Altar, we're hoping we can use it to help locate him.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Alas, so far we've had no luck. We're also scanning for energy that matches what we observed during that earthquake he caused, but again, nothing has been discovered so far.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: It seems this work will take time. I assure you though, we will find him and his master.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about that Pyromancer?

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: We've begun an investigation into their order, but we have little to go on. Hopefully we find a good lead soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well just let me know if you need my help.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: I will, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Farewell.

sections.title: If the player lost the memoir page before using it on the memoir

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Good day friend.

lines.speaker: Lord Trobin Arceuus
lines.text: Thank you for helping our people. Please accept these gifts, they are the least you deserve.
